text.content:
Total required setup time: 15-20 minutes.

The need for better security at the Afterlife has been made readily apparent, and the party has been asked to find a suitable contractor to provide it. Fortunately, a well-reputed face from the 2030s has just surfaced, and is ready to talk.

Silver Bullets

Friend Opportunity: Silvers

Silvers is a merc with a rep, and knowing her unlocks doors, in that regard - if you have her as a friend you gain 1 personal rep. She respects and quietly admires those who are quietly competent at their work, and dislikes flamboyant posers - that is, perhaps in opposition to the setting, she's about substance over style.

Enemy Opportunity: Exec Handler

Silvers' old boss wasn't bad, but the idiot she's been handed to is a manipulative control freak with a party streak that's earned a spot in her little black book after he's spent the last couple months blackmailing her into work. If players have any enemies that are execs, you can replace the existing handler with one of them.

Rep Opportunity: Good With Kids

Get Silvers' family to like you - trust me, they'll never shut up about you after that. Only one character can earn this achievement.

Gig Payout

100eb + 2 Eagles per player, with a 25eb + 1 Eagle bonus if Silvers puts in a good recommendation for the team. Also comps the players for entrance into the Afterlife's front doors and/or bar, while they're working for the club.

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Meeting Sprinter & Vido

The Afterlife is almost ready to open, but Sprinter needs a head of security she can trust and lean on to keep things stable - and she knows just who she wants.

Beat 2: Learning the Situation

Sprinter knows where Silvers is, though she wasn't easy to track down - figure out why she's still there.

Beat 3: Silvers' Boss

Silvers is none-too-eager to stay under the employ of her exec overlord, and spills the details on what's keeping her there - it's up to the players to go deal with the situation.

Beat 4: Fuel Tanks

Silvers' son has been located - go get him back.

Beat 5: Consequences

Wrapping up the mission.

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

"Good evening my little Nightcrawlers. This is Nightlight, the voice of the city, here to take the scream out of the sheets and into your ears with tones cool and clean. Whether you're coming home after a double shift at the bodega or heading out for the Night City nightlife, Nightlight says, 'stay safe out there.' Time for news.

"Our top story today is one as old as Night City is tall. Seems that a certain perfectly professional, many-armed merc with a vivacious appetite for violence has been spotted around town. Silvers the Solo, made famous in the 2030s from a long string of jobs where important people went missing and Petrochem ended up owning half of Watson. Nothing proven of course, but it seems this legendary ice queen's steamin', freezin' and breezin' around NC once again, so if someone with a wallet wants your head, well I'd just keep that hair low down to the ground.

"In other news, it seems like 6th Street's finally beating the scavs back into the back alleys of South Night City, meanin' those of you payin' protections earned something for it after all. Looks like we're all one step closer to a safer city - until the next gang war, anyways.

"Lastly, I've got a big beautiful bounty on a bottle of 1991 Cabernet, and a lot of other liquor that likely hasn't seen the light of day since Silverhand bade us his fond fairwell. A certain french fanatic seems to be stocking his cabinets, and didn't put an upper cap on the number of bottles - so if you've been hoarding that last good bottle and need to make rent this month, you know who to call.

"Up next we've got music to murder to, from Dethklok to the Dropkick Murphys. Have fun out there tonight, my little Night Citizens, and remember, never leave home without protection. Until later, this is Nightlight, and I hope I've made your night a little brighter."

In this beat, players meet up with the Afterlife's staff members once again, to learn what's next for the club after their antics a few days prior.

This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby} and @UUID[Scene.GVfVYf3mAoPg0JO5]{Fuel Tanks} scenes, along with a few environments. To begin:

  • Place tokens for @UUID[Actor.17CTAE5lKmZFHY9i]{Vido Dubois} and @UUID[Actor.C1opBA4gYUZcTiGp]{Sprinter} in the Afterlife Lobby scene, sitting at some of the furniture.

  • Place the player tokens outside the front doors in the Afterlife Lobby scene

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.VCbthP8Ez4uhtdkr]{Afterlife Exterior Environment}

Then read the following:

Another Day, Another Job

It's a cool evening in Night City, which is a sum relief after the heat and humidity of the day. The clouds from the previous job have long since burned away, leaving the city streets to soak and steam in the sunlight amid the smells of melting asphalt and boiling urine.

Each of you received a text from Vido, earlier in the day:

Bonjour, mis amis! Your next job is ready, and sooner than I expected! Please come for a visit this evening; myself and dear Sprinter will be waiting with drinks in hand and gold in the wings.

An addendum follows: "Ah yes, and as the security is now working properly, mind the door; it has a bit of an attitude towards uninvited guests, and will readily shear you in twain, should you cross without payment."

(Swap the scene to the Afterlife Lobby)

You'll recall that the doors to the Afterlife don't open automatically, and require a coin to get in. Now that security is working properly, so are the doors.

  • Every visit to the Afterlife requires at least one coin for admission; this purchase allows a user to stay on the Afterlife grounds for up to 18 hours, before being asked to pay for a room.

    • While on the premises, players are wrapped in one of the safest locations in Night City, soon to be moreso: once Silvers is recruited, she and her team will hunt down those who attack others on the Afterlife premises, delivering to them a swift and well-deserved end.

    • Meanwhile the automated defenses have at least been enough to keep out the riffraff, and the location is still relatively unknown - anyone staying the day will be safe, whether pursued by loan sharks, Militech or Maelstrom.

  • Each coin opens the doors just long enough for 2 individuals to enter; they snap shut with hydraulically-driven force after the second person, and are smart enough to recognize two people.

    • A third can sneak in behind the other two with a DV21 Stealth check

    • If players are entering the Afterlife at the request of one of its staff members, they can knock and ask Sprinter to let them in, instead of paying, though this is a breach of the Afterlife's rules.

      • Initially, this will only require a DV13 persuasion check for each player entering, assuming they look presentable. If any player befriended Sprinter, they automatically pass this check.

      • After this mission, the club will "officially" open, and this check becomes more difficult; players will need to pass a DV17 check each time instead, and supply a new reason why they aren't able to pay (or mooch off one of their party members). If Sprinter is a friend to them, this check is reduced to a DV13, though they still need a new reason each time.

      • Once the check is failed once, it's failed forever; if you don't have a coin to get in, you'll have to ask the person providing the job to meet you somewhere else.

  • If something trips the security (like a third player attempting to enter and failing their stealth check), the doors automatically snap shut.

    • Anyone in the doorway when this happens must make a reflex save (roll 1d10; a result ≤ their REF score passes)

      • On success, they move to either side of the doorway

      • On failure, they suffer 4d6 damage (reduced by body armor), a random appendage is severed (they take the damage for the critical injury), and they're placed on whichever side of the doorway they were closer to when it shut.

      • They'll also be apprehended by security, who will perform first aid to staunch the bleeding and then swiftly eject the offender (with their dismembered body part, if necessary) and warn them that if they're caught doing it again they'll lose more than just a limb.

Once Inside

It seems someone's taken the covers off of the furniture since you last visited, revealing a gorgeous array of baby-blues and whites, most of which is made from plush and comfortable synth-leather. Of the previous job's altercation with the scavs, there remains no trace.

Sprinter and the Frenchman are sitting in the same area they were the last time you met; next to Vido is an open wooden crate, and the two are chatting animatedly about a recent MMA match while sipping drinks. "Ah, there you are!" Vido calls. "Come, sit! Care for a glass of Night City's finest? I guarantee you won't have a drink like this again for free, anytime soon!"

Roleplaying Exercise - Pick Your Poison

As players sit down, they can choose to partake in the alcohol that Vido has sourced, which is soon to be used as part of the Afterlife's bar (which is still being set up downstairs). Players may name any type of alcohol, but need to provide a unique maker and a little bit of context surrounding the drink (hether it's from a local brewery or imported from a Russian oligarch's personal reserve, Vido has everything).

Those who participate may earn 1 background point if that system is being utilized, which expires at the end of the mission - otherwise, they simply gain +3 to their next check.

Once everyone's settled, Sprinter begins talking about the job.

Job Details

  • Sprinter is the hotel's general manager, taking on the various duties that keep things humming so The Owner can focus on their work. She's seeking assistance, in this regard.

  • The Afterlife needs a head of security - someone who can take care of the ne'er-do-wells who make trouble, be they inside the building or escaped and across the city.

  • It just so happens that a famous mercenary from the 2030s has been spotted around town after disappearing, years prior, and Sprinter's already made contact to work out a deal.

    • The merc's name is Silvers, and she's a bodyguard, saboteur, and professional assassin.

    • Silvers seemed amicable to the job offer, but said she needed assistance to be released from her current boss, and refused to say more without meeting the crew that would be responsible for helping her.

    • As for why she'd be so cagey, Sprinter can guess that she has a rather large bounty on her head and wants to keep a low profile.

  • Silvers has provided a discrete time and place - more specifically, an automated synthcoff shop a few blocks from the border between Watson and New Westbrook. In terms of meeting spots, its nowhere - the gangs that tagged it have probably already forgotten it exists.

    • Sprinter wants the party to go meet with Silvers, see what she wants, and get her back to the Afterlife - willingly - to discuss the details of their potential contract further.

    • Outside of those sparse details, Silvers requested a "competent team of at least - the number of party members present -" but didn't say anything further.

In this scene, the party meets Silvers for the first time, to learn what she needs from them while she gets a read on their capabilities as edgerunners.

To set up the scene, switch to the @UUID[Scene.511vQ9q4ArXVTYv6]{North New Westbrook Environment}, read through the Talking as Silvers bullets for yourself, name Silvers' Boss at your discretion, and then read the following:

North New Westbrook, Daytime

You can smell the change in the air as you leave downtown, and the cloistered, stinking humidity of the bay begins mingling with the parched winds of the Badlands. The address Sprinter gave you is on the very north edge of Night City, past the urban sprawl, where endless blocks of apartment buildings and seedy suburbs have finally turned into warehouses and industrial sprawl.

The factories here are humming as though the Red Decades had never occurred. For the businesses that own them, demand for their products has never been higher, and competition never lower. Steel refineries and CHOOH2 processors belch steam and carbon dioxide into the greyish-blue sky, and the inner-city stink of human refuse has been replaced by the ever-present smell of industrial chemicals; it makes your throat sore, and leaves you longing for a return to your concrete jungle.

Ask the party if they intend to do anything on the trip there, and once they arrive, switch the scene to @UUID[Scene.eHT9S5zSQV6IgEQw]{QwikkBreww Environment}, then read the following:

QwikkBreww

Arriving at the Synthcoff Stand you're greeted by dirty windows and gang tagging, the Reckoners, 6th Street and Maelstrom logos overriding one another across the walls. Inside, there's no workers - just like most of the district, the stand is automated, with nothing but a row of branded vendits selling scopbrew that likely expired years ago. The inside is empty except for plastic stools, tables covered in dust and ash, and a boosterganger kicking one of the machines who glares at you as he sees you, and then leaves out a side exit.

Once the party is visible in the shop, Silvers texts:

I see you. Be in momentarily. Don't do anything stupid.

The party has around a minute before Silvers arrives. The coffee bar itself is the opposite of anything special, and just on the edge of palatable.

Before long you see her approach from down the sidewalk, stepping out of a side alley. Although her hair is done up in a tight bun and she's wearing a suit that looks like it's worth more than the coffee shop, she has a pair of swords on her hips, and a pair of pistols above them. Steely silver cybereyes glint at you even across the street - oh, and the extra pair of arms subtly tucked away just behind and above the first raise eyebrows too.

Now would be a good time to show your party Silvers' art, if you haven't already.

As she enters the store, she looks over the party. "So, you the crew Sprinter sent out, I assume?"

Talking as Silvers

Silvers is direct and to the point, and doesn't waste time with chit-chat unless someone looks like they really don't belong, in which case she'll point that out and might need a little convincing that the runner responsible knows what they're doing.

  • The job itself: Silvers is essentially being blackmailed/held hostage by her current employer, an exec affiliated with Petrochem, or else a corp of your choice.

    • Formerly, she'd worked for the individual's father, and found that situation more agreeable. However, she was given to his son as a present and, when she expressed that she was ready to quit, her new boss decided to blackmail her into staying to save face.

    • Professionally, Silvers has been running black ops against SovOil for Petrochem, in exchange for the corp concealing her identity. This is part of why her new boss wants to keep her on; she's very good at her job.

  • Although she does want to keep her head low until she's safe in the Afterlife, exposure isn't why she's sticking around.

    • Rather, the exec is holding a child hostage, and if Silvers makes any kind of move, the boy will die.

    • A bit of persuasion (GM discretion) reveals that the child, Bobby, is not Silvers' own, but rather she robbed the cradle of an exec she assassinated in the 2030s.

      • The reason she took the child is because if she hadn't, it would have starved in the crib and she was worried leaving it would cause her to fall into cyberpsychosis.

      • She kept it out of a sense of human connection - she simply had no better solution for making sure the infant was safe and well cared for.

      • Silvers is not altogether happy about the weakness this has produced in her, but ruefully and begrudgingly accepts that she's a better person for it. She would die for her boy.

      • Oh, and the kid doesn't know he was a stolen baby, and she'd prefer to keep it that way.

  • The job itself is to find and recover the boy, by whatever means the party finds appropriate.

    • The boy himself is being kept hidden by some hired goons that the exec owns - but Silvers doesn't know where. If she did, she would've made the move on her own.

      • She suggests that the party go to meet her boss and get the info out of him, somehow.

    • The child is named Bobby - no last name - and Silvers describes him as about 9 years old, around 4'5", with bright green eyes and a big smile.

    • Silvers also emphasizes that if anything happens to her child, she will hunt down and kill the party for allowing him to be hurt.

      • This isn't a joke - if the players somehow get Bobby killed or he disappears into the streets of NC, Silvers will immediately kill her boss, his personal guards, and then begin hunting the players as a cyberpsycho. Time to call in MaxTac.

  • Lastly, Silvers notes that while she's not sure what state her child is in, he's been in his new home for a few months now, and she'd like to make sure he gets out with a minimum of trauma if he's being well cared for.

    • That is, she's not sure if the exec's just thrown him in a day-camp, hired a surrogate caregiver, or what, but wants to make sure he's removed without too much issue.

  • She's not sure what apparatus the exec has cooked up to ensure the child's condition is linked to her boss's own, but suspects that it involves a dead man switch on his biomonitor - and making a quick call on his internal agent from a signal number would also be trivially easy.

Once all of the above information has been given, Silvers leaves - she's taken a few minutes break to meet the party, but notes she has to get back to the office.

  • Before leaving, she shares that her team is loyal to her, but her boss doesn't fully trust them and has a pair of personal guards contracted from Militech that won't betray him.

    • She provides the party the office address, and requests that they be discrete in their visit, but that her team won't stand in their way while they're on the premises.

Once players are ready to meet with Silvers' boss or make a move for the boy, progress to beat 3 and/or 4. See Thinking Outside The Box for suggestions on how the situation can be resolved without putting anyone's life in jeopardy.

In this beat, the party must squeeze the whereabouts of Silvers' child from the clutches of her boss, one way or another.

This scene uses the @UUID[Scene.x34watvhLSZ6Fkp2]{Silvers' Office Environment} scene. To proceed:

  • Familiarize yourself with the Layout & Occupants of 166 W. Max so as to explain to your players

    • Describe it yourself, gonk, don't be lazy and dump the layout description on them.

  • Figure out how you intend to represent Silvers' boss as a character - there are no strict rules, but they do have a few character traits listed under As The Boss.

Then switch the scene to Silvers' Office Environment, and read the following:

166 W. Max Street, New Westbrook

The office that Silvers works in sticks out from New Westbrook's industrial sprawl like a sore thumb - a big, black, single-story cube of tinted windows and painted concrete. The Badlands dust which clings to the surroundings seems to ignore it, as though its very existence is aberrant to its surroundings.

Once players have entered…

Inside, a guard looks up at you from a reception desk, where they were reading from an electronic tablet and are clearly bored. "Can I help you?" They ask.

Around you are a scattering of chairs and coffee tables; on the left is a door which has been left ajar, revealing more security staff clustered around a television - from the sounds of things, they're playing a splitscreen game of an old first person shooter on an out of date console.

Next to the desk, an L shaped hallway branches, with one arm going left and disappearing behind the back of the security office, and the other going straight into the back of the building; there are what looks to be two other rooms connected to it, and the doors of both are closed.

If players get into the security office…

The room is small and packed with furniture, with a widescreen television against the wall which abuts the hallway. A few metal desks are scattered about, none of which have any personal effects on them, but most of which have various amusements - tablet computers, books, etc. Furniture is scattered about in front of the television, underneath which is a notoriously failure-prone game console from the early 2000s along with a collection of old games and controllers.

If players enter the Boss's office…

The office strikes you as kitschy, as though all the items in it were bought just because they were expensive rather than because they fit each other. A snow-white, furry rug covers the carpeted floor, atop which sits a desk that's made of redwood, with a computer atop that. There's no keyboard, though there is a monitor - evidently cyberware of some kind is used to operate it instead. Behind it, a tinted window looks out into a trash-strewn back alley and the wall of an industrial facility. In the corner is an L-shaped couch, and there's a trio of chairs in front of the desk, as well as a gaudy, throne-like leather computer chair sitting behind it.

If players enter the Boss's rec room…

The room is dominated by neon lighting, and the windows have been blacked out. It smells like sex. There's a large couch in the center of the room, sitting atop a leopard-print carpet which itself sits on a brightly patterned, extremely gaudy carpet that looks like it came straight out of an arcade in the 2020s. Around the room are scattered tables with various entertainment features, along with a small fridge and kitchenette.

Layout

The building has two entrances/exits: a pair of double sliding doors at its front, and a fire escape about halfway down the left side, at the end of a hallway.

The building itself is only four rooms; entering through the front, a lobby is lined with chairs, with a small reception desk at its back. To the left is a door to the security office, which contains most of the staff along with some furniture, a few desks, and some entertainment.

Heading down the hall that's behind and to the left of the receptionist desk, on the left is the office of Silvers' boss. On the right is the boss's rec room, which has a plush sofa, a fridge filled with prepak snacks, Smash, and liquor, and a scattering of tables holding braindance gear, gaming setups, and several hits of street drugs of your choice. How much all this junk is worth is up to you.

Occupants

The building is staffed by 1*Partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.91JT7Zncn3Sb60Ge]{RatSec Elites} - Silvers' team. There's one in the lobby, and the rest are either off-site or in the security office amusing themselves. They won't bother the player unless failing to do so would arouse the suspicion of Silvers' handler.

Speaking of, Silvers' Boss is a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.nUF7oF2uBUTM4Ur4]{RatSec Exec}, named at your discretion. They occupy their office in the back left corner, along with Silvers, and a pair of vigilant @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.6rEW0UHcCQVMShPe]{Militech Suppressors} which are loyal to the boss and only the boss.

As The Boss

Silvers' boss is a stereotypical exec trust fund (wo)manchild, with a lot of money and no sense whatsoever. Their father has given them Silvers, which they use to run black ops against SovOil, but they don't do any fighting themselves - they let Silvers' team do it instead.

  • The boss has a raging addiction to Synthcoke and Blue Glass

  • They love status, but nobody likes them - you know the type, they have lots of "friends" that they party with but would never be invited if they weren't rich.

  • They're very… dude-bro. They look down on everyone and think they're smart despite having never worked a real day in their life.

  • Bobby is being held at a Petrochem fuel depot which is owned by the wing of the company which the exec reports to.

    • The team of gangsters that are there, watching Bobby, are also siphoning off fuel from the facility and selling it to their friends, in exchange for giving the exec a 50/50 split of the profits.

    • Though the exec themself doesn't care about this development, the corporation might if they heard of it.

  • The info on where Bobby is being kept can be located in several places (see Thinking Outside the Box). Aside from the options kept there at GM discretion, it can be located in the scene:

    • On the computer in the boss's office (Netrunning checks at GM discretion; otherwise, requires a DV17 Electronics/Security to locate, and access to an interface with which to use the computer

    • Otherwise, the boss themself will likely spill the beans - the party needs only to find a way to coerce him into doing so without his realizing/caring what he's doing. This can be accomplished in 4 ways:

      • The direct approach: A DV17 Interrogation Check is required, if the Militech guards are dealt with and the boss is put into a position to be "coerced."

      • The persuasive approach: A DV24 Persuasion Check can be made to bribe the exec into releasing the information, which can be reduced to a DV15 if offered in conjunction with a bribe of 1000eb or more (this can be determined with a base roll of 11 or more in the Bribery skill after a couple of minutes of conversation, or by making a DV13 Bribery check after the same period).

        • The boss has a party to go to, and they've used up their allowance for the month.

      • The conversation approach: A DV17 Conversation Check can be made if the boss is comfortable with the presence of the person making it - E.G. drinking with them, doing drugs, they go to the same parties, etc. On success, they spill the beans.

      • The deductive approach: A DV15 Deduction check can be made after speaking with the boss for more than 20 in-game minutes, to piece together a likely area of search and just find the location yourselves.

  • If the boss is threatened, they can use their internal agent to call a predetermined number, with the call being the signal to kill Bobby.

    • Doing so only takes 1 action or 5 seconds, and only requires a voice command.

    • The signal will also automatically be sent if the boss dies, as extra incentive for Silvers' to keep them alive on the job.

    • The gangsters won't actually kill Bobby - they like him too much - but 10 minutes after the call is made, they'll disappear into Night City, never to be heard from again.

Once the players have located the info they're after, or successfully screwed the pooch on finding it, progress to Beat 4 or 5.

In this beat, the players will rescue Silvers' boy from his surrogate family/gang caretakers.

This beat takes place in the @UUID[Scene.GVfVYf3mAoPg0JO5]{Fuel Tanks} scene. To begin:

  • In the Fuel Tanks scene, place 1*Partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangsters}, 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.siXtBrzjkX0N0AIc]{Gangster Enforcers}, and 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.fut71IAOEpYyDksL]{Gangster Slumlord} around the scene, with the appropriate reinforcements waiting in the wings (recommended off the north side of the map, to be moved on later.)

    • Name the Enforcers and Slumlord; Bobby is on a first-name basis with them, and they've come to care about each other.

    • Gangster reinforcements arrive in groups of up to 1/2 partycount, and arrive on foot via the roadway.

  • For every 4 gangsters (not including reinforcements), place 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.NcmoLFKKy8hGryfN]{Generic Compact} or 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.FvEVg5aUhP5dLU0b]{Generic Truck} somewhere in the scene, each of which has 25 less max health and 5 less MOVE than they otherwise would. They're worth half (or less - GM discretion) their stated book value if they were bought, and probably even less than that if sold.

    • They're ratty and damaged, and liable to break down, but still run and haven't been tagged by any gangs. The enforcers have the keys.

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.BXWyuUclBeDdmf0b]{Bobby Silverson} in the booth next to the entrance.

    • Bobby is being entertained by at least 1 Gangster Enforcer or the Slumlord, while the remainder roam the premises.

  • Place the player tokens on the road, along with any vehicles they arrived in which didn't then depart.

  • Familiarize yourself with As The Gangsters

Then switch the scene to the Fuel Tanks, and read the following:

Nondescript Northeastern New Westbrook - Industrial Sprawl

From the office, it's not a long drive to get to where Bobby's being held. Funny, how just a few blocks is separating one of the most powerful edgerunnerrs in the city from her child. From the housing to factories and warehouses, eventually those give way as well, to an endless field of fuel infrastructure, power lines, derelict oil fields and pipelines connecting tanks - the fuel infrastructure capital of the area, and the reserves that keep the cars, trains and aeros running.

The streets are largely deserted. Every so often you'll spot maintenance crews moving between the sights, going from dusty and decrepit portable buildings to banged-up work vans and trucks, but there's no civilians and no gangs - and little question as to why. Well-worn and weathered autoguns and drone systems watch the properties, keeping their weapons trained on you as you move by, ever vigilant for potential trespassers. The RatSec forces aren't worth battling when you can just rob the gas stations or hijack the tanker trucks instead, and the result is relative peace.

Before long you've reached the location you found. Six fuel tanks are enclosed by a chainlink fence, abutting the side of the road. You don't spot any of the automated defenses you've seen on any of the other properties - perhaps because of this, the space is occupied. You see bodies moving around, with guns, guarding the joint. One is watching the road.

As The Gangsters

The gangsters are working for Silvers' boss, selling fuel to their friends at a discount in exchange for cutting the exec in on the profits. As a result, when the players arrive, the goons will think they're prospective customers rather than an immediate threat.

  • When players arrive, so long as the gangsters don't know they're coming, the enforcer that's not watching Bobby will approach them, offering to sell them fuel "at a good price."

    • A couple of the boosters will likewise be watching the party from inside the compound, to make sure they don't make trouble.

    • Most of the gangsters will act with stereotypical swagger, but won't show outright hostility unless addressed poorly or the characters are somehow affiliated with another gang that they'd have reason to be opposed to.

  • Bobby is being kept inside the guard post at the edge of the property, playing a game with the other enforcer. Although being paid to watch him, Bobby and the gangoons have bonded over the interim months, and come to care about each other.

    • Bobby knows the gangsters by name, and will be miserable and terrified by the players if his friends are brought to harm.

    • Likewise, the gangsters have groomed Bobby into one of them - he wears a 'do rag, walks with a swagger, wears a crew cut t-shirt, carries a paintball gun and talks like they do.

    • If a gunfight breaks out or Bobby is otherwise terrified, he will run into the city and vanish.

      • If a DV15 tracking check is made once he vanishes, he can be located less than a block away. Otherwise, this is a fail-state.

  • How the players liberate Bobby is up to them, but here's some suggested paths:

    • Use of Force: Although it isn't ideal for Bobby, he has technically been kidnapped - blowing away the gangsters guarding him and just taking the kid works fine, though it may affect how Silvers treats the players.

    • Intimidation: If players succeed a facedown against the slumlord on the scene, they can attempt a DV15 Interrogation Check to try and intimidate him into releasing the kid without a fight.

      • The slumlord will attempt to initiate a facedown when the players arrive once he's interacting with them - use REP+ 1/2 lowdown (rounded up) for the roll.

      • Note that if the players pressure the gangsters too hard, a fight might break out.

    • Persuasion: The players can attempt a DV21 Persuasion Check to appeal to the thugs' better nature, and reunite Bobby (whom they do care about) with his mother without bloodshed.

      • This check is reduced to a DV17 if a bribe of 300eb is made while explaining that their employer isn't long for the world and that Bobby will be well taken care of. It can instead be reduced to this amount if the players promise that those so-inclined (in particular the enforcer that the kid is playing with on arrival) can see Bobby once he's back in his mother's custody.

    • Acting: The players can attempt a DV17 Acting Check to lie to the gangsters, that their boss wants Bobby back at the office for one reason or another.

      • This check can be skipped if the players use forgery to fake a notice from the boss in question.

  • If one or more player characters find out that Bobby and his captors are, in fact, friends, and successfully remove Bobby by force without convincing him to the otherwise, those characters suffer 1d4 humanity loss on the way back to Silvers. You heartless bastard!

This beat uses the @UUID[Scene.x34watvhLSZ6Fkp2]{Silvers' Office Environment} scene. To proceed, switch back to it, identify which ending(s) are appropriate for the players' actions, then read the associated resources.

On Return

Another day in Night City…

If Bobby Is Returned Alive, Unharmed, And His Captors Are Still Alive & On Good Terms… (Good Ending)

Bobby babbles happily on the way back to Silvers' office, sharing the various adventures he's gotten up to with his friends at the fuel tanks.

It's possible he's taken to one PC in particular - if so, now would be a good time to describe how and why.

On arrival back at the office, Silvers and her boss are in the lobby, along with some of Silvers' team and the Militech guards. They pause mid-sentence when you walk in - the boss's eyes widen in recognition, and quicker than you can see, Silvers' blades have flashed out of their sheathes. The boss gasps and chokes as their neck is sliced open in a splash of crimson, and as if they were just waiting for the signal, her team springs into action and incapacitates the militech accompanyment.

"Mom!" Bobby shouts, seemingly unphased by the violence. Silvers' demeanor cracks a bit as she smiles lovingly down at him, before kneeling down to embrace him in a hug.

  • You should consider this to be the best ending, awarding players with their associated bonuses as outlined in the mission summary.

  • Once Bobby and Silvers are reunited, Silvers expresses her thanks, and notes that her name was probably broadcast to every bounty hunter in the city the moment she flatlined her boss. She and her crew have a couple of groundcars, and they'll be off to the Afterlife before anyone notices they're gone.

  • If allowed, one or more of the gangsters might have traveled back with Bobby, and received a handshake from Silvers followed by a swift punch in the jaw, though she seems amenable enough to their continued friendship with Bobby.

  • If a PC managed to make friends with Silvers, she needs help moving into her hotel room at the Afterlife from the tiny corporate flat that her boss provided to her, and would appreciate some assistance.

  • See "Service Unlocked: Silver Bullets"

If Bobby Is Injured, Or Terrified/Heartbroken Following The Death/Excommunication Of His Friends… (Neutral Ending)

Insensate tears and sobbing have only just started to die down as you finally return to Silvers' office. Bobby is still blubbering quietly, and it's clear to see that even though he'll survive the day, he's far from okay.

Inside, in the lobby, Silvers and her boss are engaged in some kind of argument. Silvers' eyes widen in time with her employer's as Bobby is brought inside, and she draws a sword in one hand a moment later, lashing out furiously at her former owner, who's stabbed in the gut before she pops an arm and blows his head open with a pistol shot. Before the body's hit the ground, her team are splattering the walls with his Militech guards - against that much coordinated firepower, they didn't stand a chance.

"Mom!" Seeming to pay no mind to the carnage, Bobby rushes forwards with tears streaking his face, and Silvers drops her sword to kneel and embrace him as he does. Her own calm demeanor shatters in time, as a look of intense worry crosses her face. "Bobby! I'm so glad you're safe; what happened?"

  • The neutral ending - Silvers will not blame the players for Bobby's trauma, though they won't earn their bonus and might've taken a few too many shots for the payout to have been worthwhile.

    • She puts the blame squarely on the shoulders of her dead former superior, instead.

  • Once Bobby and Silvers are reunited, Silvers expresses her thanks, and notes that her name was probably broadcast to every bounty hunter in the city the moment she flatlined her boss. She and her crew have a couple of groundcars, and they'll be off to the Afterlife before anyone notices they're gone.

  • If a PC managed to make friends with Silvers, she needs help moving into her hotel room at the Afterlife from the tiny corporate flat that her boss provided to her, and would appreciate some assistance.

  • See "Service Unlocked: Silver Bullets"

If Bobby Is Killed, Or Disappears Into The City… (Bad Ending)

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

Hey y'all, Nightlight here, taking the scream out of the sheets and into the ears for those of you who don't have time to read. Let's talk news.

Remember that special gal I was telling you about? The one-woman army that the mercs call Silvers? Seems she's back in NC after all, and on the warpath - NCPD investigation was conducted today, and it looks like she was the one who strung those poor gonks up on the North Central Bridge. If you ended up with a bit of intestine on your windshield on the commute home today, you know who to thank.

No word on what her motive is, but it looks like she's got Maelstrom in her sights at the moment - several more bodies have been found, stripped of their chrome and left to rot on the streets outside the Old City Center. Sounds like our silver lady is arming up to take on someone, though - whoever she's after, Nightlight wishes you the best and hopes you sleep with your gun in your lap.

Now to music.

  • The worst ending - players will not earn their rewards and Silvers will relentlessly pursue them.

    • Vido/Sprinter will not blame the party for their failure - shit happens, after all - though they won't get paid for it.

  • Silvers has killed her former employer, their guards, and some of her former team as she went over the edge into cyberpsychosis on news of her son's death/"disappearance."

    • With her boss out of the way, she views the players as responsible - roll her into the scene, once a gig, until she's either dead or the original party has been killed.

    • You may also upgrade her sheet with up to 2000eb of combat-ready cyberware, at your discretion.

  • If Silvers appears in the remainder of the campaign's content, she's instead absent. If she's essential, use Sprinter instead.

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

If you haven't already, you should also make the associated journal (in this case it's @UUID[JournalEntry.ell4mv1S6wxd79lv]{01a. Included Service: Security Team}) visible to your players - you can do so by right clicking it, selecting "Configure Ownership", and changing the "All Players" dropdown to "Observer."

Sometimes, you and your players might find that a few autoguns won't be enough to discourage bad actors within the Afterlife's hallowed halls. Silvers has been recruited to fix that problem.

Silvers In The Afterlife

Silvers has taken up residence in one of the Afterlife's hotel rooms, along with her son - she's staying there indefinitely in exchange for her services, which was part of her negotiations with The Owner.

While on the grounds, she primarily acts as a bouncer for the establishment while on-duty, and spends time homeschooling her son when not. She rarely leaves the premises except on business, on account of the 60k EB bounty on her head.

Service: Silver Bullets

While on the Afterlife's staff, Silvers acts as the muscle of the establishment, taking out the trash. If an individual or organization has committed violent acts on the Afterlife's grounds, she confers with Sprinter and The Owner to reach a verdict:

  • If it was a minor offense, the individual responsible might be excommunicated - their Eagles become worthless and a bounty is placed on their head - if an NPC, the party might even be able to pursue it.

  • Otherwise, Silvers and her team will hunt the individual responsible down, and eliminate them.

    • Aside from Silvers herself, her team is comprised of 1*partycount RatSec Elites. Each one may be equipped with a Torrell & Chang armorjack and up to 1000eb of extra gear at GM discretion.

  • Obviously, individuals should feel free to defend themselves without repercussions, if attacked.

Is your party struggling to come up with a good way to approach the office without jeopardizing the mission?

Some parties relish the challenge of an unexpected obstacle. Others, though…

I've had it both ways - in one playtest, my party spent 20 minutes brainstorming ideas for how to get into the office and save Silvers' kid without putting anyone in harm's way. And in another, the problem seemed insurmountable until I provided them with some in-character knowledge as to how they might approach to the otherwise.

If you as the GM have some ideas and your players are stumped, try having them make tactics or deduction checks for general suggestions, or use more particular skills (business, streetwise, local expert, etc.) based on character backgrounds.

Regardless of how you deliver it, though, here's some role suggestions for different plan ideas.

  • Fixers could try and source the information on where Bobby is being held without ever interacting with the exec at all.

  • Execs from the same company could try and intimidate Silvers' boss into giving up the information, or could represent a rival company looking to buy out Silvers' contract.

  • Netrunners could hack the computer system to find where Bobby is being held, if they're given the opportunity.

  • Solos could create a distraction, drawing the exec out of the office to make life easier.

  • Medias could sus out blackmail or rumors which lead to leverage over the exec.

  • Lawmen could threaten the exec in a legal capacity, assuming they have the power and authority to do so.

And so on.
